description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated figure in traditional attire, possibly of East Asian origin, with a tall, ornate headdress and flowing robes. The figure is positioned on a rectangular stone pedestal, which features engraved plaques with inscriptions on the front and side. The sculpture is rendered in a dark, metallic material, giving it a solemn and dignified appearance. The base is constructed from a lighter, rougher stone, contrasting with the smooth, glossy finish of the figure itself. The front plaque contains both Japanese and English text, suggesting a commemorative or historical significance. The overall form is solid and monumental, designed to be a prominent feature in a public or ceremonial space.
description: A seated figure in traditional attire with a tall headdress, rendered in dark metal atop a stone pedestal with inscribed plaques. The sculpture has a glossy finish and a dignified, commemorative presence.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, hands, and headdress of the seated figure, as well as the main inscribed plaque on the pedestal.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the figure, the draping of the robes, and a secondary plaque on the pedestal.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress and robes, with the plain stone base visible and no inscriptions.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the figure's profile and the texture of the stone base, with no visible plaques.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure in traditional attire
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is installed on a stone pedestal, likely in an outdoor public or commemorative setting, as suggested by the presence of plaques and the robust construction.
